 {"id":"bd-y3vd","title":"Implement has_release_workflow() detection","description":"# Release Workflow Detection

## Parent Epic: bd-mkoc (Agent Sweep Command Implementation)

## Purpose
Determine if repo should have release automation (Phase 3).

## Implementation

```bash
has_release_workflow() {
    local repo_path="$1"
    local workflows_dir="$repo_path/.github/workflows"
    
    # Check explicit per-repo config first
    local repo_config="$repo_path/.ru/agent-sweep.conf"
    if [[ -f "$repo_config" ]]; then
        source "$repo_config"
        case "${AGENT_SWEEP_RELEASE_STRATEGY:-}" in
            never) return 1 ;;
            tag-only|gh-release|auto) return 0 ;;
        esac
    fi
    
    # Check user-level per-repo config
    local repo_name=$(basename "$repo_path")
    local user_config="$RU_CONFIG_DIR/agent-sweep.d/${repo_name}.conf"
    if [[ -f "$user_config" ]]; then
        source "$user_config"
        case "${AGENT_SWEEP_RELEASE_STRATEGY:-}" in
            never) return 1 ;;
            tag-only|gh-release|auto) return 0 ;;
        esac
    fi
    
    # Use gh API if available
    if command -v gh &>/dev/null; then
        local remote_url
        remote_url=$(git -C "$repo_path" remote get-url origin 2>/dev/null)
        if [[ -n "$remote_url" ]] && gh workflow list -R "$remote_url" 2>/dev/null | grep -qi "release\|deploy\|publish"; then
            return 0
        fi
    fi
    
    # Fallback: check workflow files
    [[ -d "$workflows_dir" ]] || return 1
    grep -riqE "(on:|tags:|release:|workflow_dispatch:)" "$workflows_dir"/*.yml 2>/dev/null
}
```

## Release Strategy Values
- never: No releases
- auto: Agent proposes, ru validates (default)
- tag-only: Create tag, no GH release
- gh-release: Tag + GH release + monitor Actions","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-06T16:57:21.4043331-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-06T16:57:21.4043331-05:00"}

 {"id":"bd-yk9p","title":"Implement ntm_wait_completion()","description":"# Wait for Agent Completion

## Parent Epic: bd-9o2h (NTM Driver Integration Layer)

## Purpose
Wait for Claude Code agent to complete work and return to idle state.

## Implementation

```bash
ntm_wait_completion() {
    local session="$1"
    local timeout="${2:-300}"
    local output exit_code
    
    output=$(ntm --robot-wait="$session" \
        --condition=idle \
        --wait-timeout="${timeout}s" \
        --exit-on-error 2>&1)
    exit_code=$?
    
    echo "$output"
    return $exit_code
}
```

## ntm Flags Used
- --robot-wait=SESSION: Target session
- --condition=idle: Wait for agents to be idle (not generating)
- --wait-timeout=Ns: Maximum wait time
- --exit-on-error: Return early if error pattern detected

## Exit Codes
- 0: Condition met (agent idle)
- 1: Timeout exceeded
- 2: Error (check error_code)
- 3: Agent error detected (via pattern match)

## Response Schema (success)
```json
{
  "success": true,
  "session": "ru_sweep_myrepo_12345",
  "condition": "idle",
  "waited_seconds": 45.2,
  "agents": [
    {
      "pane": "0.1",
      "state": "WAITING",
      "met_at": "2026-01-06T15:35:00Z",
      "agent_type": "claude"
    }
  ]
}
```

## Response Schema (timeout)
```json
{
  "success": false,
  "error": "Timeout waiting for condition",
  "error_code": "TIMEOUT",
  "hint": "Increase timeout or check agent status with --robot-activity",
  "agents_pending": ["0.1"]
}
```

## State Detection
ntm uses velocity tracking + 53 regex patterns:
- Velocity >10 chars/sec = GENERATING
- Velocity <1 chars/sec + idle pattern = WAITING
- 0 chars/sec for 5+ seconds = COMPLETE
- Error pattern match = ERROR

## Timeout Guidelines
- Phase 1 (understanding): 180s default
- Phase 2 (commits): 300s default
- Phase 3 (release): 600s default
- Configurable via --phase{1,2,3}-timeout","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-06T16:49:43.712151674-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-06T19:07:30.415581876-05:00","closed_at":"2026-01-06T19:07:30.415581876-05:00","close_reason":"Implemented ntm_wait_completion() with idle condition wait and error detection at lines 6385-6408. Also added ntm_get_activity() for polling.","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-yk9p","depends_on_id":"bd-6kme","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-06T16:58:19.121931136-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-yk9p","depends_on_id":"bd-h6rv","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-06T17:28:52.709336347-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
-{"id":"bd-yrod","title":"Implement session cleanup and trap handlers","description":"# Session Cleanup and Trap Handlers

## Parent Epic: bd-kvu5 (Error Handling & Recovery)

## Purpose
Implement session cleanup function and trap handlers for graceful shutdown.

## Functions to Implement

### cleanup_agent_sweep_sessions()
```bash
# Kill all agent-sweep sessions for this process
# Respects --keep-sessions flag
cleanup_agent_sweep_sessions() {
    [[ "$KEEP_SESSIONS" == "true" ]] && return 0

    local pattern="ru_sweep_*_$$"
    local sessions
    sessions=$(tmux list-sessions -F "#{session_name}" 2>/dev/null | grep -E "$pattern" || true)

    for session in $sessions; do
        log_verbose "Cleaning up session: $session"
        tmux kill-session -t "$session" 2>/dev/null || true
    done
}
```

### setup_agent_sweep_traps()
```bash
# Set up trap handlers for graceful shutdown
setup_agent_sweep_traps() {
    trap 'agent_sweep_handle_interrupt' INT TERM
    trap 'agent_sweep_handle_exit' EXIT
}

agent_sweep_handle_interrupt() {
    log_warn "Interrupted - saving state and cleaning up..."
    save_agent_sweep_state "interrupted"
    cleanup_agent_sweep_sessions
    exit 5
}

agent_sweep_handle_exit() {
    local exit_code=$?
    # Only cleanup if not keeping sessions
    if [[ "$exit_code" -ne 0 ]] && [[ "$KEEP_SESSIONS_ON_FAIL" == "true" ]]; then
        log_info "Keeping sessions for debugging (--keep-sessions-on-fail)"
    else
        cleanup_agent_sweep_sessions
    fi
}
```

### map_ntm_error_to_exit_code()
```bash
# Map ntm error codes to ru exit codes
# Args: $1=ntm_error_code
# Returns: ru exit code
map_ntm_error_to_exit_code() {
    local ntm_error="$1"

    case "$ntm_error" in
        TIMEOUT)           echo 1 ;;  # Partial failure
        RESOURCE_BUSY)     echo 1 ;;  # Retry-able
        SESSION_NOT_FOUND) echo 3 ;;  # System error
        PANE_NOT_FOUND)    echo 3 ;;  # System error
        INTERNAL_ERROR)    echo 3 ;;  # System error
        PERMISSION_DENIED) echo 3 ;;  # System error
        DEPENDENCY_MISSING) echo 3 ;; # System error
        INVALID_FLAG)      echo 4 ;;  # Bad args
        NOT_IMPLEMENTED)   echo 4 ;;  # Bad args
        *)                 echo 1 ;;  # Default partial failure
    esac
}
```

## Integration
- Called from cmd_agent_sweep() at startup for trap setup
- Called on exit/interrupt for cleanup
- Error mapping used by ntm driver functions

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Sessions cleaned up on normal exit
- [ ] Sessions preserved with --keep-sessions
- [ ] Sessions preserved on failure with --keep-sessions-on-fail
- [ ] State saved on interrupt (Ctrl+C)
- [ ] Trap handlers don't interfere with normal operation
- [ ] Error code mapping covers all ntm error codes","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-06T17:55:10.439602142-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-06T17:55:10.439602142-05:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-yrod","depends_on_id":"bd-hkmt","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-06T18:04:46.932195647-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}

### cleanup_agent_sweep_sessions()

# Kill all agent-sweep sessions for this process

cleanup_agent_sweep_sessions() {

# Set up trap handlers for graceful shutdown

 {"id":"bd-z89z","title":"Implement atomic state persistence with flock","description":"# Task: Implement Atomic State Persistence

## Purpose
Store review state (outcomes, questions, runs) in JSON files with atomic writes and flock-based locking to prevent corruption from concurrent access or interrupted writes.

## Background: Why Atomic Writes?
- Ctrl+C during write = corrupted JSON
- Concurrent reads during write = partial data
- Power failure = lost state
- Solution: write to temp file, then atomic mv

## State Files

### 1. review-state.json
```json
{
  "version": 2,
  "repos": {
    "owner/repo": {
      "last_review": "2025-01-04T10:30:00Z",
      "last_review_run_id": "abc123",
      "issues_reviewed": 3,
      "prs_reviewed": 1,
      "issues_resolved": 2,
      "outcome": "completed",
      "duration_seconds": 847,
      "digest_hash": "sha256:..."
    }
  },
  "items": {
    "owner/repo#issue-42": {
      "type": "issue",
      "number": 42,
      "last_review": "2025-01-04T10:30:00Z",
      "outcome": "fixed",
      "notes": "Path handling fixed for Windows"
    }
  },
  "runs": {
    "abc123": {
      "started_at": "2025-01-04T10:00:00Z",
      "completed_at": "2025-01-04T11:30:00Z",
      "repos_processed": 8,
      "items_processed": 14,
      "questions_asked": 12,
      "mode": "ntm"
    }
  }
}
```

### 2. review-questions.json
```json
{
  "version": 1,
  "questions": [
    {
      "id": "q_abc123",
      "run_id": "run_xyz",
      "repo": "owner/repo",
      "session_id": "ru-review-owner-repo",
      "pane_id": "1",
      "context": "Should I refactor...",
      "options": ["a) Minimal fix", "b) Full refactor"],
      "priority": "normal",
      "detected_at": "2025-01-04T10:45:00Z",
      "status": "pending"
    }
  ]
}
```

## Implementation

### State Lock
```bash
# Global state lock (separate from review session lock)
STATE_LOCK_FD=201

acquire_state_lock() {
    local lock_file="$RU_STATE_DIR/state.lock"
    exec 201>"$lock_file"
    flock -x 201
}

release_state_lock() {
    flock -u 201 2>/dev/null || true
}

# Scoped lock helper
with_state_lock() {
    acquire_state_lock
    "$@"
    local rc=$?
    release_state_lock
    return $rc
}
```

### Atomic JSON Write
```bash
write_json_atomic() {
    local file="$1"
    local content="$2"
    local tmp_file="${file}.tmp.$$"
    
    # Write to temp file
    echo "$content" > "$tmp_file"
    
    # Validate JSON before committing
    if ! jq empty "$tmp_file" 2>/dev/null; then
        rm -f "$tmp_file"
        log_error "Invalid JSON, refusing to write: $file"
        return 1
    fi
    
    # Atomic move
    mv "$tmp_file" "$file"
}
```

### Read with Lock
```bash
read_state_json() {
    local file="$1"
    local default="${2:-{}}"
    
    acquire_state_lock
    if [[ -f "$file" ]]; then
        cat "$file"
    else
        echo "$default"
    fi
    release_state_lock
}
```

### Update State
```bash
update_review_state() {
    local updates="$1"  # jq filter
    local state_file="$RU_STATE_DIR/review-state.json"
    
    acquire_state_lock
    
    local current
    if [[ -f "$state_file" ]]; then
        current=$(cat "$state_file")
    else
        current='{"version":2,"repos":{},"items":{},"runs":{}}'
    fi
    
    local updated
    updated=$(echo "$current" | jq "$updates")
    
    write_json_atomic "$state_file" "$updated"
    
    release_state_lock
}
```

### Record Item Outcome
```bash
record_item_outcome() {
    local repo_id="$1"
    local item_type="$2"
    local number="$3"
    local outcome="$4"
    local notes="$5"
    
    local item_key="${repo_id}#${item_type}-${number}"
    local now
    now=$(date -u +%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%SZ)
    
    update_review_state "
        .items[\"$item_key\"] = {
            \"type\": \"$item_type\",
            \"number\": $number,
            \"last_review\": \"$now\",
            \"outcome\": \"$outcome\",
            \"notes\": $(echo "$notes" | jq -R .)
        }
    "
}
```

## Checkpoint State (for Resume)
```bash
checkpoint_review_state() {
    local checkpoint_file="$RU_STATE_DIR/review-checkpoint.json"
    
    local state
    state=$(cat << EOF
{
  "version": 1,
  "timestamp": "$(date -u +%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%SZ)",
  "run_id": "$REVIEW_RUN_ID",
  "mode": "$REVIEW_MODE",
  "repos_total": ${#REVIEW_REPOS[@]},
  "repos_completed": ${#COMPLETED_REPOS[@]},
  "repos_pending": $(printf '%s\n' "${PENDING_REPOS[@]}" | jq -R . | jq -s .),
  "questions_pending": $(cat "$RU_STATE_DIR/review-questions.json" 2>/dev/null || echo '{"questions":[]}')
}
EOF
)
    
    with_state_lock write_json_atomic "$checkpoint_file" "$state"
}
```

## Cleanup Old State
```bash
cleanup_old_review_state() {
    local max_age_days="${1:-30}"
    local state_dir="$RU_STATE_DIR"
    
    # Clean old worktrees
    find "$state_dir/worktrees" -maxdepth 1 -type d -mtime "+$max_age_days" \
        -exec rm -rf {} \; 2>/dev/null || true
    
    # Prune old runs from state file
    local cutoff
    cutoff=$(date -u -d "$max_age_days days ago" +%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%SZ 2>/dev/null || \
             date -u -v-${max_age_days}d +%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%SZ)
    
    update_review_state "
        .runs |= with_entries(select(.value.started_at > \"$cutoff\"))
    "
}
```

## Testing
- Write, crash, verify temp file cleaned up
- Concurrent writes don't corrupt
- Read during write gets consistent data
- Checkpoint and resume works
- Malformed JSON rejected

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] All state writes are atomic (temp + mv)
- [ ] flock prevents concurrent write corruption
- [ ] JSON validated before commit
- [ ] Checkpoint captures full state
- [ ] Old state cleaned up automatically","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-04T15:18:52.733473052-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-04T16:45:19.174289659-05:00","closed_at":"2026-01-04T16:45:19.174289659-05:00","close_reason":"Implemented all state persistence functions with flock-based locking and atomic JSON writes. Functions: acquire/release_state_lock, write_json_atomic, read_state_json, update_review_state, record_item_outcome, record_repo_outcome, record_review_run, checkpoint_review_state, load_review_checkpoint, clear_review_checkpoint, is_recently_reviewed, cleanup_old_review_state. All acceptance criteria met.","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-z89z","depends_on_id":"bd-mnu9","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-04T15:44:52.911714994-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}

Add a synchronous governor_update() helper and warn when using start_governor_background(); also clean up skipped-question count parsing.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-01-04T19:29:56.519227789-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-04T19:30:22.624067697-05:00","closed_at":"2026-01-04T19:30:22.624067697-05:00","close_reason":"Added governor_update() helper + warning for background governor; simplified skipped question count","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-zimg","depends_on_id":"bd-gptu","type":"discovered-from","created_at":"2026-01-04T19:29:56.544070417-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
